Clone the DeepSpeedExamples repository or obtain the script autotuning/hf/gpt2-xl/test_tune.sh from the specified commit ff9a0234cf22dd9af03c7aa8037fb9143adca6
Ensure you have a machine with at least 6 GPUs (e.g., 6 x NVIDIA 3070 Ti) for testing, or adjust the number of GPUs in the script accordingly
Set up a Python environment with Python 3.8+ and install required dependencies: pip install torch torchvision deepspeed transformers
Clone the HuggingFace transformers repository (https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.git) and install it via pip install . inside the cloned directory
Create a new directory, e.g. ~/test, and copy the contents of autotuning/hf/gpt2-xl/test_tune.sh into a new file named test_tune.sh inside it
Modify test_tune.sh to set HF_PATH=../ (pointing to the transformers directory) and NGPUS=6 to match your GPU count
Make test_tune.sh executable by running chmod +x test_tune.sh
Run ./test_tune.sh tune to start the autotuning process using DeepSpeed and GPT-2 XL model
Observe the console output logs carefully for initialization messages of DeepSpeed autotuner and resource manager allocating localhost GPUs 0-5
Wait for the autotuning to start launching the profile_model_info experiment with resource localhost:0,1,2,3,4,5
Verify that the script attempts to SSH into localhost for distributed resource management, and verify SSH permission issues if any (this is a separate warning, not the main bug)
Eventually, during the model info profile run, observe the error message: 'ParquetConfig.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'token''
Confirm the stack trace or error message points to DeepSpeed autotuner internals trying to initialize ParquetConfig with a 'token' argument which it does not accept
Verify that no optimal DeepSpeed configuration is found and autotuning ends prematurely after this error
Assert that the error is reproducible by re-running the same sequence with identical environment and parameters
Optionally, validate the Python environment versions of deepspeed and transformers to confirm ParquetConfig's signature mismatch
Document that the bug is specifically triggered by running the autotuning script test_tune.sh with the GPT-2 XL model and DeepSpeed autotuner enabled
